title: Safety Promotion & Training
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
Safety Promotion & Training
NYU Shanghai is committed to creating a safe and supportive environment where all students, faculty, and staff can thrive. Through our safety promotion and training programs, we provide the campus community with the knowledge, skills, and resources needed to stay informed, prepared, and engaged in personal and community safety...
We offer a range of interactive trainings, awareness campaigns, and safety resources designed to help you respond confidently in emergencies, prevent common risks, and contribute to a culture of care on campus.
Whether you’re new to NYU Shanghai or looking to stay up-to-date, we invite you to explore our workshops, learn practical safety tips, and take part in building a resilient and proactive campus community.
Safety 101
Orientation PPT (PDF)
Safety Week is an annual NYU Shanghai tradition that promotes campus-wide safety through a week of themed events and workshops. Covering topics like CPR, self-defense, fire safety, scam prevention, and more, the program combines education with interactive activities, games, and giveaways. Open to all students, faculty, and staff, Safety Week fosters a stronger, safer, and more informed community.
Recap:2025 Campus Safety Week Has Officially Started! 平安从今天开始——校园安全周启幕！
Full Highlights Recap: NYU Shanghai Campus Safety Week 2025 (Video)
2025 Campus Safety Week Highlights: Self-Defense Workshop
2025 Campus Safety Week Highlights: Fire Department Engagement
2025 Campus Safety Week Highlights: VR Immersive Lab Safety Experience
2025 Campus Safety Week Highlights: First-Aid, CPR and AED Workshop
Campus Safety Week Tackles Everything from Cyber-Security to CPR
The Safety Ambassador Program engages students as peer leaders in promoting safety awareness across campus. Ambassadors receive training, assist with events, and serve as a vital link between Campus Safety and the student body. The program builds leadership skills while supporting a culture of shared responsibility and preparedness.
Recap:Travel Safety Tabling Recap
Live, Thrive, Flourish Expo Recap
Ready to Fall for My Trick? 校园防诈骗宣传活动回顾
Easy Money For Liking Videos?-How This Empty Pockets Worldwide？
Stay Alert, Stay Safe. Fire Awareness Starts with YOU
Winter Fire and Power Safety Promotion Tabling
Safety Training
Campus Safety offers training programs to equip the NYU Shanghai community with essential knowledge in areas such as fire safety, emergency response, and first aid. These sessions help build readiness, promote personal responsibility, and ensure a safer campus for all.
Safety Education 101 (Orientation)
General Safety Awareness Training
First Aid and CPR Training
AED Operation
Personal Safety Training
Fire Safety Training
Stay informed and prepared with our Safety Tips Resource Hub — your go-to guide for practical advice on staying safe on and off campus. Explore resources covering emergency preparedness, personal security, travel safety, and more to help you make informed decisions every day.
Fire Safety in Residence Hall
Fire Safety - Easy Guide on How to Use a Fire Blanket
Travel Safety Checklist
Traffic Safety - Travel Smart Stay Safe
Interpretation of Shanghai’s New Drone Regulations
Protect Yourself from Impersonation Fraud
Fire Safety - R.A.C.E Framework
Safety Tip - How to report an incident
Scam Prevention - Anti-Scam 101
Safety Tips - Safe Holiday
All You Need to Know Before Using Drones
Card Policy and Visitor Registration
Power Safety & Power Bank Recycle Program
Takeout Check-out
Apartment Rental Safety Tips
Our Customized Safety Workshops are designed to meet the unique needs of our community. Whether you’re part of a student organization, department, or staff team, we’ll tailor the training to address your specific concerns and schedule.
Click here to request a customized training session.
Feedback
Thank you for taking a moment to share your thoughts. Your feedback is essential in helping us strengthen safety across our university community. Whether you’re a student, faculty member, or staff, your voice matters.
Upcoming Events
